data.title: Spirit AeroSystems Non-GAAP EPS of -$3.03, revenue of $1.5B misses by $400M
data.description:
• Spirit AeroSystems press release (NYSE:SPR): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS may not be comparable to consensus of $0.15.
  • Revenue of $1.47B (+2% Y/Y) misses by $430M.
  • Spirit's revenue in the third quarter of 2024 increased from the same period of 2023, primarily due to higher production activities on most Commercial programs and higher Defense and Space revenues, partially offset by lower production volume on the Boeing 737 program.
  • Overall deliveries were consistent in the third quarters of 2024 and 2023, with 332 shipsets delivered in both periods.
  • Spirit's backlog at the end of the third quarter of 2024 was approximately $48 billion, which includes work packages on all commercial platforms in the Airbus and Boeing backlog.
  • Shares -4% PM.

data.title: Teck Resources Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.60, revenue of C$2.86B
data.description:
• Teck Resources press release (NYSE:TECK): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.60.
  • Revenue of C$2.86B (+43.7% Y/Y).
  • Copper production with 114,500 tonnes in the third quarter, of which 52,500 tonnes were from QB. Production at QB continues to ramp-up and we expect to be operating at full throughput rates by the end of 2024.
  • 2024 Guidance – Summary Current
    Production Guidance
    Copper (000’s tonnes) 420 - 455
    Zinc (000’s tonnes) 565 - 630
    Refined zinc (000’s tonnes) 240 - 250
    Sales Guidance – Q4 2024
    Red Dog zinc in concentrate sales (000’s tonnes) 155 - 185
    Unit Cost Guidance
    Copper net cash unit costs (US$/lb.) 1.90 - 2.30
    Zinc net cash unit costs (US$/lb.) 0.45 - 0.55

data.title: Thursday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
8:30 AM Chicago Fed National Activity Index

A monthly report by the Chicago Federal Reserve Bank that tracks economic activity in the 7th district, which is comprised of Indiana, Iowa, Illinois, Michigan and Wisconsin. The index is useful in tracking economic growth and identifying potential inflation. The September Chicago Fed national Activity index is expected to jump to 0.20 from 0.12 in August.

8:30 AM Initial Jobless Claims

Initial Jobless Claims measures the number of individuals who filed for unemployment insurance for the first time during the past week. The number of individuals filing for unemployment benefits in the U.S. is expected to jump to 245K from 241K.

8:45 AM Fed’s Hammack: "Inflation: Drivers and Dynamics 2024"

Cleveland Federal Reserve Bank President Beth Hammack to give welcome remarks before the "Inflation: Drivers and Dynamics 2024" event hosted by the Center for Inflation Research at the Federal Reserve Bank of Cleveland and the European Central Bank.

9:45 AM PMI Composite Flash

The Composite PMI Index measures the activity level of purchasing managers in the both sectors. Flash figures are released approximately 6 business days prior to the end of the month and intend to get a preliminary reading of the final figure, based on approximately 85% of survey responses. The S&P Global US Composite PMI came in at 54 in September 2024 from 54.6 in August.

10:00 AM New Home Sales

New Home Sales measures the annualized number of new single-family homes that were sold during the previous month. This report tends to have more impact when it's released ahead of Existing Home Sales because the reports are tightly correlated. The index is expected to edge higher to 717K in September from 716K in August.

10:30 AM EIA Natural Gas Inventory

The Energy Information Administration Natural Gas Storage report measures the change in the number of cubic feet of natural gas held in underground storage over the prior week. US utilities added 76 billion cubic feet of gas into storage during the week ended October 11, 2024, below market expectations of a 80 bcf rise.

11:00 AM Kansas City Fed Mfg Survey

The survey monitors about 300 manufacturing plants selected according to geographic distribution, industry mix, and size in the Tenth District. Survey results reveal changes in several indicators of manufacturing activity, including production and shipments, and identify changes in prices of raw materials and finished products. The Kansas City Fed's Manufacturing Production index plummeted to -18 in September 2024, down from 6 in August, marking a 14-month low and falling far short of market expectations of 9. 0

1:00 PM 5-Yr TIPS Auction

The treasury will hold an auction of $24B, 5-Year TIPS.

4:30 PM Fed Balance Sheet

The Fed balance sheet is a weekly report listing the assets and liabilities of the Federal Reserve System.

Click here to see the weekly economic calendar.

data.title: Boeing factory workers reject contract, continuing six-week strike
data.description: • Boeing (NYSE:BA) factory workers voted late Wednesday to reject the company's contract offer that would have ended a six-week strike.

data.title: Oil futures slide as U.S. crude stockpiles rose more than expected
data.description:
Crude oil futures snapped a two-day positive streak on Wednesday as a bigger than expected build in U.S. crude inventories sparked demand concerns, pushing Middle East tensions to the back burner.

Commercial crude oil stockpiles excluding the Strategic Petroleum Reserve rose by 5.5M barrels to 426M barrels in the week ended October 18 and were ~4% below the five-year average for the time of year, according to new data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration.

Analysts surveyed by The Wall Street Journal had predicted crude stockpiles would rise by 800K barrels.

U.S. refineries ran at 89.5% of capacity, up 1.8 percentage points from the previous week, compared to a small decline forecast in the WSJ survey, yielding a build in gasoline stocks of 878K barrels to 213.6M barrels, ~3% below the five-year average, compared to expectations of a 1.3M-barrel decline.

The report added to persistent concerns over China's demand outlook and prospects of a global supply surplus next year that sent oil futures falling more than 7% last week.

Also pressuring oil prices on Wednesday, the dollar index rose to its highest since late July; a firmer dollar can hurt demand for dollar-denominated oil from buyers using other currencies.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for December delivery closed -1.3% to $70.77/bbl, while front-month December Brent (CO1:COM) ended -1.4% to $74.96/bbl.

U.S. natural gas futures (NG1:COM) rose ahead of the weekly domestic inventory report that is expected to show a contraction in the surplus to 143B cf from 163B cf the previous week; Nymex front-month settled +1.3% at $2.342/MMBtu.

ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (NYSEARCA:UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

The third-warmest October in the U.S. in more than 60 years and warm forecasts for early November have significantly weighed on natural gas prices, with Nymex gas down 21% month-to-date, a move Goldman Sachs analysts say is appropriately sized.

However, Goldman says it maintains its $3.10/MMBtu Henry Hub forecast for now, "given that the potential weather-driven swings in demand over the coming weeks and months are significantly larger than what we observed thus far in October."

data.title: Gold rally reflects growth of dollar alternatives in warning to West, El-Erian says
data.description:
Gold prices slid more than 1% Wednesday after initially hitting yet another record high, as a stronger dollar and higher U.S. Treasury yields likely prompted some amount of profit-taking.

Following sustained support from safe-haven demand tied to the Middle East conflict and the upcoming U.S. presidential election, SP Angel analysts said the BRICS summit is also in the spotlight, with de-dollarization talks underway set to boost gold further.

Money managers have raised their gold bullish positions this week, with short-only positions falling, and exchange-traded fund holdings also continue to grow, hovering ~84M oz, SP Angel added.

On Monday and Tuesday, SPDR Gold Trust (NYSEARCA:GLD), the largest physical gold ETF, attracted ~$580M of net positive inflows, according to Samer Hasn of XS.com.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for October delivery closed -1.1% to $2714.40/oz, after setting a fresh record of $2,772.60/oz earlier in the session, and front-month October silver (XAGUSD:CUR) settled -3.4% to $33.640/oz.

ETFs: (GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

Western countries should take surging gold prices more seriously, former Pimco CEO Mohamed El-Erian said this week, as the metal's move reflects increasing interest in alternatives to the dollar-based financial system.

"Something strange has happened to the price of gold over the past year," El-Erian wrote in the Financial Times. "In setting one record level after the other, it seems to have decoupled from its traditional historical influencers, such as interest rates, inflation and the dollar."

"What is at stake here is not just the erosion of the dollar's dominant role but also a gradual change in the operation of the global system," according to El-Erian.

"No other currency or payment system is able and willing to displace the dollar at the core of the system and there is a practical limit to reserve diversification," he said. "But an increasing number of little pipes are being built to go around this core; and a growing number of countries are interested and increasingly involved."
